hSub: Premium motorcycle expeditions across the world's most breathtaking archipelago. Bali, Komodo, Sumba — real roads, real adventure.

tours.name: Bali to Komodo
tours.desc: The ride that defines us. 12 days across 4 islands — from Bali's spiritual heart to the prehistoric shores of Komodo.

tours.name: 7 Islands Hopping
tours.desc: 13 days of tropical paradise. Adventure mixed with relaxation — turquoise waters, hidden beaches, island roads forever.

tours.name: Sumba Challenge
tours.desc: The wildest island in Indonesia. Raw landscapes, traditional villages, and roads almost no one has ridden.

tours.name: Bali to Jakarta
tours.desc: Cross the Ring of Fire. Fourteen days through Java's volcanic spine — ancient temples, jungle roads and the crossing from paradise to the capital.

reviews.q: Thirteen days, seven islands, zero regrets. I have done trips across Southeast Asia but nothing comes close to this. B2K made it look easy.
reviews.name: Tyler B.

reviews.q: I was skeptical about a guided motorcycle tour but this converted me. Small group, world-class roads, genuinely off-the-beaten-path. Already planning the Komodo route.
reviews.name: Sarah K.

reviews.q: Did the Bali to Komodo route for my 40th birthday. The ferry crossing with the bikes, the Komodo dragons, the empty volcanic roads — I will never forget it.
reviews.name: James M.

reviews.q: Coming from Australia I was looking for something genuinely adventurous. This delivered. The team knows every road, every stop, every sunset spot.
reviews.name: Marcus R.

reviews.q: Third time with B2K and I keep coming back. It does not feel like a tour — it feels like riding with friends who know every hidden road in Indonesia.
reviews.name: Marco K.

reviews.q: From Vancouver to Bali — best decision I made last year. The Sumbawa roads are empty and perfect. Already locked in for 2026.
reviews.name: Chloe D.

reviews.q: As a rider from Texas I thought I had seen good roads. Indonesia blew my mind. B2K handles everything so you just focus on the ride.
reviews.name: David W.

reviews.q: Sydney to Bali and back with the best two weeks in between. The moment we crested the hill above Labuan Bajo I had tears in my eyes.
reviews.name: Emma T.

reviews.q: I was nervous about my riding level but the guide adapted everything perfectly. The landscapes are out of this world and the group energy was incredible.
reviews.name: Sophie L.

komodoPage.heroNote: self-guided · all inclusive · excl. intl. flights
komodoPage.barSpots: Only a few spots left. Book now →

itinerary.summary: First night in Bali. Balinese dinner on the beach with the rest of the group.
acts: Arrival, bike check-in and mandatory orientation briefing
Welcome dinner at a high-quality local partner venue on the beach

itinerary.summary: A coastal route north and the first test of escaping Bali traffic. Buddhist temples and a local educational project.
acts: Hindu purification ceremony at the revered Pura Kelecung temple
Visit the Escuela Torrevieja foundation — 500,000 IDR donation per pilot
Ride through the lush rice terraces of Tabanan

itinerary.summary: Batur Volcano, rice terraces, and hot springs before reaching the cultural heart of Bali.
acts: Ride towards the magnificent Mount Batur volcano
Batur hot springs — complimentary 30-minute thermal massage

itinerary.summary: Boat crossing to Lombok, paradisiacal beaches and discovery of Mandalika. You cross the Wallace Line.
acts: Early ferry crossing from Bali to Lombok — crossing the famous Wallace Line
Ride through Lombok capital region to Senggigi overnight

itinerary.summary: A day of rest on a traffic-free island with crystal-clear waters and white sand.
acts: Lombok coastal explorer — scenic coasts and inland roads
Gili Trawangan free time, snorkeling, relax before the demanding Sumbawa legs

itinerary.summary: Back to Lombok, hidden waterfalls in the jungle and coastal roads. Ferry to Sumbawa — the true wilderness begins.
acts: Ferry crossing from Lombok to Sumbawa
Ride the challenging Sumbawa South Coast — far from tourist crowds

itinerary.summary: Remote roads and crazy viewpoints. The wild heart of the archipelago.
acts: Tambora Volcano Expedition — ride past the legendary Tambora volcano
Remote jungle roads, dramatic viewpoints and authentic local villages

itinerary.summary: The longest and most demanding day. Jungle, dirt roads and hidden villages.
acts: Most challenging day of the tour — isolated terrain, off-road sections
Arrive at Moyo Utara base camp before the whale shark encounter

itinerary.summary: Snorkel with whale sharks in the turquoise waters of Moyo Utara / Saleh Bay.
acts: Whale Shark Snorkeling at remote Saleh Bay
Evening: board the long-haul night ferry to Flores

itinerary.summary: Island route with two options: technical and intense, or cultural and panoramic among crop terraces.
acts: Final ride to Komodo gateway — arrive in Flores at Labuan Bajo
Bike drop-off and preparation for the final boat excursion

itinerary.summary: Encounter with the Komodo dragons and the incredible pink sand beach.
acts: Board boat to Komodo National Park — iconic Komodo Dragon trek
Pink Sand Beach — one of the rarest beaches in the world

itinerary.summary: Return by plane to Bali. End the party at one of the best beach clubs.
acts: Final group breakfast and transfer to Labuan Bajo airport
Beach club celebration — the 12-day Bali to Komodo Challenge is complete

faq.q: What is the Bali to Komodo Challenge?
faq.a: A 12-day, 11-night motorcycle adventure covering 1,259 kilometers across 6 major islands: Bali, Lombok, Sumbawa, Flores, Moyo and Komodo. Designed as a deep cultural and geological exploration, crossing the famous Wallace Line and concluding with a visit to the Komodo Dragons.

faq.q: What riding experience do I need?
faq.a: Recommended for riders experienced in long-distance touring and comfortable with varied conditions including heavy traffic in Bali and challenging isolated terrain in Sumbawa. Personal international travel insurance is mandatory for all participants.
Purification ceremony at Pura Kelecung in Bali · Tambora Volcano National Park · Whale Shark snorkeling at Saleh Bay · Komodo Dragons trek · Pink Sand Beach · Beach club finale in Bali.
Your main luggage is transported daily in a dedicated support vehicle driven by our mechanic. You only carry a daypack with essentials on your motorcycle — ensuring a comfortable and focused ride.
Honda CB150X for single riders under 100kg, or the Kawasaki Versys 250 for two riders or solo riders over 100kg. All bikes subject to mandatory nightly mechanic check.
Not included in the base price. Two options: Full-Risk Insurance policy for $282 (covers all bike damages), or a Refundable Security Deposit of $1,090 upon arrival (returned if no damages occur).
Yes. Maximum of 12 motorcycles per tour and a maximum group size of 24 people. This ensures a quality, personal experience for every rider.

faq.q: Can I book a call first?
faq.a: Absolutely. Write to us on WhatsApp and we will schedule a free 30-minute video call so you can talk directly with our team before committing. No pressure.

itinerary.summary: Land in paradise. Check in to your boutique hotel in the heart of Seminyak, then watch the sun melt into the Indian Ocean.
Rice terraces, sacred water temples and the thrill of lazy river tubing through lush Balinese jungle.
acts: Taman Beji Griya Temple — water purification

itinerary.summary: Emerald rice terraces, cascading waterfalls and a fresh fish feast with the Bali Sea as your backdrop.

itinerary.imgAlt: Mount Batur volcano and lava fields, Bali
itinerary.summary: Driving on lava fields, soaking in volcanic hot springs and sleeping in a glamping tent perched above the caldera.
Bali's spiritual capital. The mother temple, a hidden waterfall and a Balinese massage before a premium dinner.

itinerary.imgAlt: Sacred Monkey Forest and Ayung River, Ubud
itinerary.summary: White-water rafting on the Ayung River, the famous Monkey Forest and a ridgeline walk above the jungle.

itinerary.imgAlt: Gili Islands turquoise beach and clear water
itinerary.summary: Cross from Asia into Oceania. Arrive in paradise — no motorised vehicles, turquoise water, a beach massage and a night market under the stars.
acts: Sanur port — crossing Asia to Oceania
Arrive Gili Islands — transfer by horse carriage (no cars!)

itinerary.summary: A private diving excursion across all three Gilis, snorkelling with 4,000+ species and exploring the surreal underwater sculptures of Gili Meno.
acts: Gili Meno — underwater statues & sunken bikes

itinerary.imgAlt: Kelingking Beach cliff viewpoint, Nusa Penida
itinerary.summary: Kelingking Beach — the most photographed beach in Indonesia. Dramatic cliffs, hidden lagoons and Indonesia's most iconic landscape.
acts: Kelingking Beach — most photographed in Indonesia

itinerary.imgAlt: Diamond Beach and Manta Point, Nusa Penida
itinerary.summary: Snorkelling with manta rays at Manta Point, Diamond Beach and a massage with the Pacific as your view.
acts: Pura Goa Giri Putri cave temple

itinerary.summary: The iconic Yellow Bridge, mangrove forests by stand-up paddle and seaweed farms stretching into the turquoise lagoon.
Hidden beaches, Devil's Tear and a speedboat back to Bali for one last sunset at Kuta.

itinerary.imgAlt: Aerial view of Bali, departure morning
itinerary.summary: One last morning in paradise. Optional helicopter tour over Bali before your flight home — or just soak it all in one last time.

faq.a: Everything: boutique hotel accommodation, all meals, every activity on the itinerary, inter-island transfers, Balinese massages, temple ceremonies, surfing lesson, river rafting, snorkelling and cooking class. Not included: international flights, visa fees and personal travel insurance.

faq.q: Do I need to ride a motorcycle?
faq.a: No. The tour offers four transport options: shared motorcycle (Bike × 2), solo motorcycle (Bike × 1), self-drive car, or private car with driver. You choose what fits your comfort level.

faq.q: What level of fitness is required?
faq.a: Medium. The itinerary includes river rafting, surfing lessons, ridge walks and snorkelling sessions. The driving days are long but comfortable, especially in the car options. You don't need to be an athlete — just curious and ready for adventure.

Small boutique hotels carefully selected for character and location — beachfront in Tibubiu, Bali Sea views in Depeha, glamping on lava fields in Batur and boutique stays in Ubud, the Gilis and the Nusas.

faq.q: Do I need a visa for Indonesia?
faq.a: Most European passport holders can enter Indonesia visa-free for up to 30 days. For longer stays a Visa on Arrival is available. Always check the latest requirements for your nationality before booking.

faq.q: How does the deposit and payment work?
faq.a: A $1,000 deposit secures your spot. The remaining balance is due 60 days before departure. You can pay by card, Apple Pay, Google Pay or SEPA bank transfer (Wise).

faq.q: Can I join as a solo traveller?
faq.a: Absolutely. Choose Bike × 1 or a car option to travel at your own pace. Solo travellers are very welcome — you'll meet the rest of the group along the way.

faq.q: What is the Gili Islands horse carriage transfer?
faq.a: The Gilis ban all motorised vehicles — the only transport is horse-drawn carriage (cidomo) or bicycle. Three islands, each with a completely different vibe: Trawangan for parties, Meno for honeymooners and Air for divers. Underwater statues and sunken bikes make for unforgettable snorkelling.
